modes.title: Helper-only
modes.claim: codexhx is tooling: DTOs, codecs, schema fingerprints, fixture transforms, reports, and haxe.rust pressure tests. It does not run Codex turns.
entryCriteria: G1 compiler scaffold and doctor gates pass for portable and metal profiles.
Any used upstream/Cafex schemas have fixture or fingerprint evidence.
No runtime, process, sandbox, model, or TUI authority is exposed.
exitCriteria: All helper outputs are reproducible from pinned upstream or Cafex sources.
Unsupported runtime surfaces are documented as not applicable.
Generated Rust remains disposable and is not presented as a runtime artifact.
completionGate: Every helper surface has a committed fixture, fingerprint, or report.
Every skipped upstream test is classified as outside helper-only scope.
No user-facing command can imply runtime replacement.
rollbackPath: Remove helper commands from the calling workflow or revert the single helper package/pin.
Delete generated outputs; do not migrate persistent state.
operatorImpact: Operators may see additional reports or diagnostics only when helper commands are invoked.
developerImpact: Developers maintain Haxe fixtures and haxe.rust pins.
Compiler gaps found here are filed as generic haxe.rust issues.
contractRequirements: Schema fingerprints must match the pinned source or be intentionally accepted in a decision record.
Fixture transforms must be deterministic and credential-free.

modes.title: Sidecar/headless
modes.claim: codexhx can run selected credential-free or controlled headless Codex-compatible flows beside upstream Codex.
entryCriteria: G2 upstream protocol/DTO subset and G3 headless one-turn gates pass.
Native tool/state gates used by the sidecar fail closed by default.
exitCriteria: Selected headless workflows pass fixture parity in Haxe and generated Cargo gates.
State and transcript artifacts are normalized and replayable.
The sidecar can be disabled without changing upstream Codex runtime behavior.
completionGate: Every sidecar command has start/status/transcript/error fixture evidence.
Provider/model behavior is mockable or controlled; no basic gate requires live credentials.
Every skipped upstream runtime test is mapped to unsupported, native, or future work.
rollbackPath: Disable the sidecar integration flag or remove the sidecar binary from the caller path.
Route traffic back to upstream Codex/Cafex.
Preserve sidecar state for diagnostics, then delete it after handoff if no migration is required.
operatorImpact: Operators get a separate process or command surface with its own structured diagnostics.
Failure should fall back to upstream Codex/Cafex rather than partially continuing inside codexhx.
developerImpact: Developers maintain protocol adapters, runtime fixtures, state normalization, and generated Cargo gates.
Unsupported live UI and host-effect surfaces stay explicit.
contractRequirements: App-server/debug JSONL protocol shape must match selected upstream contracts.
Tool, sandbox, process, and state wrappers must fail closed before mutation is enabled.

modes.title: Selected adapter-slice replacement
modes.claim: codexhx may replace named Cafex adapter slices while upstream Codex or the Cafex fork still owns the remaining runtime.
entryCriteria: Sidecar/headless completion gate is green for the slice dependencies.
G5 Cafex contract subset passes for every selected slice.
The Cafex hxrust seam ledger marks every selected row supported and every unselected row unsupported, native, or out of scope.
The HXCX-6.1 friction comparison shows positive maintenance value for the selected slice.
exitCriteria: Each selected slice has contract fixtures, rollback commands, operator diagnostics, and owner sign-off.
No selected slice depends on an unsupported live TUI, restart, queue, sandbox, or credentialed-provider path unless that path is a typed native boundary.
Cafex fork behavior can be restored without data loss.
completionGate: Every selected seam-ledger row is supported with implementation, fixture, and harness evidence.
Every adjacent unselected seam has a fail-closed diagnostic or explicit native owner.
Cafetera contract tests for the selected slice are passing or have accepted not-applicable records.
rollbackPath: Flip the selected slice routing back to the Cafex fork implementation.
Retain generated receipts/state snapshots for comparison.
Do not delete Cafex fork patch support until at least one replacement-review record accepts the slice.
operatorImpact: Operators may run mixed ownership: codexhx for selected adapter files/receipts, Cafex fork for live runtime.
Diagnostics must identify which runtime owns each failed request.
developerImpact: Developers maintain the adapter boundary and contract fixtures for each selected slice.
New haxe.rust limitations are fixed in haxe.rust generically, not with codexhx-only compiler behavior.
contractRequirements: Cafex request/receipt schemas and newline/pretty JSON conventions must remain exact.
Unknown schemas and unsupported live operations must fail closed.
Adapter code must not leak Cafex-only behavior into upstream-shaped core modules.

modes.title: Broader replacement candidate
modes.claim: codexhx can replace larger Codex/Cafex runtime surfaces for an explicitly bounded distribution.
entryCriteria: Selected adapter-slice completion gate is green for all replacement dependencies.
All upstream tests for the claimed surface pass, are ported with equivalent assertions, or have accepted not-applicable records.
All Cafex seam-ledger rows are supported, explicitly native, or accepted as out of scope in a decision record.
Security, licensing, distribution, rollback, haxe.rust production-readiness, and support-cost reviews are complete.
exitCriteria: No unclassified upstream test gaps remain for the claimed surface.
No unclassified Cafex seam gaps remain for the claimed surface.
Operators can disable or roll back the replacement without orphaned state or unclear ownership.
haxe.rust updates are pinned, reproducible, and upstreamable.
completionGate: Upstream tests passing is necessary but not sufficient.
The unsupported-surface ledger for the claimed rollout mode is empty or each remaining item has an accepted native/adapted boundary.
Operational drills prove rollback, diagnostics, and state preservation.
Decision records accept residual security/licensing/support risks.
rollbackPath: Pin and retain the last known-good upstream Codex/Cafex runtime for immediate restoration.
Keep state migration reversible or run in shadow mode until reverse migration is proven.
Disable codexhx routing globally before attempting partial repair.
operatorImpact: Operators depend on codexhx for runtime behavior inside the claimed surface.
Runbooks must cover version pins, diagnostics, rollback, and mixed-version state.
developerImpact: Developers maintain broad parity against upstream Codex and Cafex plus haxe.rust production gates.
Upstream drift and compiler drift become release-blocking risks.
contractRequirements: Upstream protocol, app-server, config, runtime, tools, state, and security contracts must be green for the claimed surface.
Cafex/Cafetera contracts must be green or explicitly native/adapted.
No contract may be skipped by routing around codexhx without a decision record.
